{{Quote|This parchment scroll supposedly contains the rambling meditations of Kooroo, an ancient sage and holy visionary who subscribed to a long-forgotten, mystical religion.|Nibao Glumm|<ref name="MosEisley">{{GamerCite|7|Secrets of Mos Eisley}}</ref>}}
'''Kooroo''' was an ancient sage and visionary. Supposedly the author of [[Meditation/Legends|meditations]] contained in the scroll known as the [[Teachings of Kooroo]], Kooroo came to be regarded as the spiritual leader of the cult known as the [[Fellowship of Kooroo]]. The cult considered the ancient structures known as the [[Shrine of Kooroo|Shrines of Kooroo]], among other sites throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], to be a legacy of Kooroo; however, [[Scholar/Legends|scholars]] considered the existence of an actual connection between Kooroo and the shrines to be unclear.

==Biography==
{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''[…]'' we do know that followers venerate the shrines as signs or relics from their spiritual leader, Kooroo.|TriNebulon News report by Andor Javin|<ref name="Journal13">{{JournalCite|13|Galaxywide NewsNets}}</ref>}}
[[File:ShrineofKooroo-Journal14.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Shrines of Kooroo were believed by the Fellowship of Kooroo to be relics or signs from Kooroo.]]
Kooroo was an ancient<ref name="MosEisley" /> being<ref name="EnterUnknown">''[[Enter the Unknown]]''</ref> who was a sage and a visionary as well as an adherent of a long-forgotten mystical religion. It was claimed that the parchment scroll known as the [[Teachings of Kooroo]], which resided in [[Shiin's Library]] in the [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] [[City/Legends|settlement]] on<ref name="MosEisley" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]<ref name="MosEisley" /> at some point by [[85 BBY/Legends|85 BBY]],<ref name="date">{{GamerCite|7|Secrets of Mos Eisley}} states that scroll known as the [[Teachings of Kooroo]] was held in [[Shiin's Library]] in the [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] [[City/Legends|city]], the latter of which was established in [[85 BBY/Legends|85 BBY]], according to ''[[Secrets of Tatooine]]''.</ref> contained Kooroo's [[Meditation/Legends|meditations]]. [[Nibao Glumm]], a native of Mos Eisley, characterized the supposed meditations of the scroll as "rambling."<ref name="MosEisley" />

Eventually, Kooroo came to be regarded as the spiritual leader of the [[Fellowship of Kooroo]], an ancient cult of mysterious origins active during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. The Fellowship believed that certain landmarks and shrines throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], including the ancient stone structures known as the [[Shrine of Kooroo|Shrines of Kooroo]], were relics or signs from Kooroo. Members of the Fellowship undertook a [[Pilgrimage of Kooroo|pilgrimage]] to those sites,<ref name="Platt" /> where they meditated in order to attain the "enlightenment of Kooroo," believed by many to refer to [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] abilities. The investigative [[Journalist/Legends|reporter]] [[Andor Javin]] mentioned Kooroo in a [[TriNebulon News/Legends|TriNebulon News]] report<ref name="Journal13" /> in [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]].<ref name="2 ABY">{{JournalCite|13|Galaxywide NewsNets}} — [[Andor Javin]] transmitted his [[NewsNet]] report on the date 38:1:15, which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], per the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]].</ref>

By [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]],<ref name="11 ABY">{{JournalCite|14|From the Files of Corellia Antilles}} is set "shortly after the re-establishment of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]" following the [[Battle of Onderon (Galactic Civil War)|final death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]].</ref> general consensus among [[Scholar/Legends|scholars]] was that the Shrines of Kooroo were built by an advanced [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]] known as the [[Kooroo (species)|Kooroo]]. However, the purpose of the structures<ref name="Journal14">{{JournalCite|14|From the Files of Corellia Antilles}}</ref> and the existence of an actual connection between the shrines and the being named Kooroo<ref name="EnterUnknown" /> were still unclear by that [[Time/Legends|time]],<ref name="Journal14" /> and even the name "Kooroo" was conjecture<ref name="TEA" /> of obscure origin.<ref name="Journal14" />

==Behind the scenes==
The being Kooroo was first mentioned in the [[1995]] [[West End Games]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] sourcebook ''[[Platt's Starport Guide]]'', which was authored by [[Peter M. Schweighofer|Peter Schweighofer]].<ref name="Platt" />
